channel.title: The Grant Desk - Visual & Media Arts, NL, next 90 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Visual & Media Arts, NL, next 90 days.

item.title: Stimuleringsfonds: Activity Programme Grant 2027-2028 - EUR 50,000 to EUR 125,000 per year for the 2027 and 2028 calendar years - deadline 15 Jun 2026
item.description:
Stimuleringsfonds Creatieve Industrie (Creative Industries Fund NL)

Location: Netherlands

Award: EUR 50,000 to EUR 125,000 per year for the 2027 and 2028 calendar years

Duration: Two years, 2027 and 2028

Deadline: 15 Jun 2026

Two-year activity-programme funding from the Creative Industries Fund NL for organisations active in design, digital culture or architecture. Supports a coherent multi-year programme of activities running across 2027 and 2028 (rather than single projects). The funding underwrites a portion of the organisation's annual programme budget, giving recipients planning stability over the two-year period. Application window 13 May - 15 June 2026. EUR 50,000 to EUR 125,000 per year per organisation, depending on the scale and ambition of the proposed programme. ELIGIBILITY: cultural and creative-industries organisations established in the Netherlands working in design, digital culture or architecture; a distinct scheme from the Fund's annual project grants, festival rounds and voucher schemes.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Stroom Den Haag: PRO Grants 2026 (Research / Projects / Programmes / Investment) - Four schemes - PRO Research, PRO Projects, PRO Programmes, and PRO Investment - with amounts typically EUR 2,500 to EUR 20,000+ depending on scheme and proposal. - deadline 31 May 2026
item.description:
Stroom Den Haag

Location: The Hague, Netherlands (eligibility restricted to professional visual artists registered with Stroom in the municipality of The Hague)

Award: Four schemes - PRO Research, PRO Projects, PRO Programmes, and PRO Investment - with amounts typically EUR 2,500 to EUR 20,000+ depending on scheme and proposal.

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Stroom Den Haag's PRO grants support professional visual artists registered with Stroom in The Hague through four schemes: PRO Research (research and development for new work), PRO Projects (project realisation), PRO Programmes (multi-stage artistic programmes spanning research, production and presentation), and PRO Investment (investment in artistic practice, e.g. equipment, studio, professional development). Three remaining 2026 application rounds: 31 May, 6 September and 25 October 2026. ELIGIBILITY: professional visual artists registered with Stroom in the municipality of The Hague (tightly geographic). Amounts vary by scheme and proposal, typically EUR 2,500 to EUR 20,000+.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Pictoright Fonds: 2026 Project Funding Rounds - Up to €35,000 per project. Applications above €5,000 must show substantial co-financing from other sources. - deadline 07 Jun 2026
item.description:
Pictoright Fonds

Location: Netherlands (NL-based visual makers)

Award: Up to €35,000 per project. Applications above €5,000 must show substantial co-financing from other sources.

Duration: Per project

Deadline: 07 Jun 2026

Pictoright's collective rights fund supports projects with a social, cultural or educational purpose serving a broad group of professional visual makers in design/illustration, photography, and fine art working/residing in the Netherlands. Six to seven rounds per year; remaining 2026 deadlines: 7 June, 23 August, 4 October, 8 November. Decisions communicated within ~6 weeks of each deadline. Maximum grant €35,000; for requests above €5,000 the project must be substantially co-financed by other sources. The remit is collective benefit (publications, infrastructure, sector-wide research, exhibitions, education, professional development for the field), not individual practice grants, so projects need to articulate how they serve other visual makers, not just the applicant. Worth tracking for any artist-organised research, publication, exhibition, training or open-source-toolkit project benefitting the Dutch visual-maker community.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Call for Proposals: Alex Adriaansens Residency 2026 - €7,500 - deadline 01 Jun 2026
item.description:
V2_ Lab for the Unstable Media

Location: Rotterdam, Netherlands

Award: €7,500

Duration: 3 months (starts 1 September 2026)

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Residency honouring V2_ co-founder Alex Adriaansens, aimed at upcoming curators, festival/programme initiators, community organisers and cultural practitioners in media art, digital cultures and related fields who are starting their professional careers. Duos or teams may apply (sharing the grant). No thematic requirement; applicants choose their own research focus and have access to V2_'s extensive archive. Apply with a letter of motivation (max 2 pages), short CV (max 1 page) and a specific research question (max 1 page) via the linked Google form, or email v2@v2.nl if you do not have a Google account. A jury of V2_ team members decides. Results will be presented in Rotterdam at a jointly organised event (e.g. expert meeting), or via an essay, or another format proposed by the resident.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Mondriaan Fund: Awareness of the History of Slavery - Funded (see grant page) - deadline 16 Jul 2026
item.description:
Mondriaan Fund

Location: Netherlands

Award: Funded (see grant page)

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: 16 Jul 2026

Support for artistic plans addressing awareness of the history of slavery. Open to individuals and organisations.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
